rows.title: Voluntary Public Access and Habitat Incentive Program competition for Federal fiscal year (FY) 2026
rows.shortSummary: NRCS is offering up to $52 million in competitive grant funding through the Voluntary Public Access-Habitat Incentive Program to support state and Tribal governments in creating or enhancing programs that incentivize private landowners to open farm, ranch, and forest land for public hunting, fishing, and wildlife-dependent recreation.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Applicant must be a state or Tribal government entity
Program must encourage voluntary public access on privately held land

rows.title: FY25 Center of Excellence-University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (Supplemental)
rows.shortSummary: Supplemental funding opportunity for the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Center of Excellence to advance research and development in passenger and freight rail transportation. Supports basic and applied research, education, workforce development, and technology transfer related to rail safety, efficiency, reliability, and sustainability.
keyRequirements: Eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Estimated Fiscal Year 2026 Adult Education and Family Literacy Act State Award Amounts
rows.managingAgency: Office of Career Technical and Adult Education
rows.shortSummary: This program provides FY 2026 state allocations under the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act (AEFLA) to support adult education and family literacy services. States receive estimated award amounts to fund eligible adult education programs and initiatives.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Must review and comply with full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) requirements

rows.title: F26AS00013 FY 2026 Clean Vessel Act (CVA) Notice of Funding Opportunity
rows.shortSummary: The Clean Vessel Act Grant Program provides cost-sharing awards to state and territorial agencies to install and maintain recreational boating facilities that prevent waste disposal into public waterways. Funding comes from the Sport Fish Restoration and Boating Trust Fund and aims to improve recreational boating opportunities while protecting water quality.
keyRequirements: Must be a designated agency of a U.S. state, territory, or the District of Columbia
Must register with SAM.gov prior to application submission
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the full Notice of Funding Opportunity

rows.title: High-performance Optimized Recycled Nuclear Isotopes for Gen IV reactors (HORNIG)
rows.shortSummary: ARPA-E funding opportunity supporting research and development of high-performance recycled nuclear isotopes for next-generation Gen IV nuclear reactors. This program aims to advance nuclear energy technology through innovative isotope optimization.
keyRequirements: Must be eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Full compliance with NOFO detailed requirements required

rows.title: WMATA Transportation – Shuttle Services Route A90
rows.shortSummary: This program funds contractors to provide comprehensive bus transportation services for Department of War personnel, specifically establishing and operating an express shuttle bus service (Route A90) between the Pentagon Transit Center and Mark Center Station facility.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Must review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Strengthening U.S. Studies in Higher Education in Argentina
rows.managingAgency:
Esteban R Carbone
Resource Coordination Specialist
rows.shortSummary: This program provides funding to strengthen U.S. Studies programs in higher education institutions in Argentina, promoting mutual understanding and academic collaboration. It supports structured, interdisciplinary education on U.S. society, governance, history, and culture.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Assistive Technology Program for Farmers with Disabilities
rows.shortSummary: The AgrAbility program provides funding and technical assistance to support individuals with disabilities, including veterans, engaged in agricultural enterprises. Through cooperative demonstration projects at state and regional levels, the program helps farmers with disabilities become more successful and strengthens rural communities.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Engaged in agricultural enterprise or supporting farmers with disabilities

rows.title: 2026 Alaska Marine Education and Training Mini-Grant Program
rows.shortSummary: The 2026 Alaska Marine Education and Training Mini-Grant Program supports workforce development and training in marine-related professions, aquaculture, maritime operations, and seafood safety. The program funds projects that enhance competitiveness in Alaska's seafood industry through education, innovation, and consumer outreach.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application submission
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
Full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) review required for detailed eligibility criteria

rows.title: INSPIRING GENERATIONS OF NEW INNOVATORS TO IMPACT TECHNOLOGIES IN ENERGY 2026 (IGNIITE 2026)
rows.shortSummary: IGNIITE 2026 is an ARPA-E funding opportunity designed to support innovative technologies in energy development. Applicants must submit through the designated portal with proper registration to compete for research and development funding.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Register with SAM.gov prior to application submission
Submit application materials through ARPA-E eXCHANGE portal
Meet posted application deadline
Review and comply with detailed requirements in the Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO)

rows.title: U.S. Mission New Zealand  Annual Program Statement
rows.shortSummary: The U.S. Department of State's Mission New Zealand announces an open competition for organizations to implement programs that advance U.S.-New Zealand economic, scientific, and cultural ties. Projects should build capacity to strengthen the bilateral relationship across various specified areas.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: U.S. Mission Public Diplomacy  Grants Program – Cook Islands & Niue
rows.shortSummary: The U.S. Department of State's U.S. Mission New Zealand offers grants to advance U.S.-Cook Islands and U.S.-Niue maritime cooperation, economic and cultural ties. The program supports local solutions and builds capacity to increase economic linkages with the United States.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: 2026 Alumni Engagement Innovation Fund, Bosnia and Herzegovina
rows.shortSummary: The 2026 Alumni Engagement Innovation Fund provides grants to U.S. government exchange program alumni in Bosnia and Herzegovina to develop and implement community-benefit projects aligned with U.S. policy objectives. All projects must celebrate Freedom 250 and be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline.
keyRequirements: Must be eligible applicant as defined in opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Project must celebrate and promote Freedom 250
Project must align with U.S. policy objectives and benefit local communities

rows.title: BJA FY 25 Invited to Apply- Transforming Prison Cultures, Climates, and Spaces
rows.shortSummary: BJA FY 2025 funding opportunity for state departments of corrections to implement the Safe Inside Initiative, which aims to improve prison safety, security, and reduce recidivism while addressing workforce challenges.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Must register with SAM.gov prior to application submission
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
State department of corrections or eligible correctional agency

rows.title: AEIF:  Celebrating Freedom 250 and Advancing USA-ROC
rows.shortSummary: The U.S. Department of State's Embassy Brazzaville is launching a competition to implement programs advancing U.S. foreign policy priorities in the Republic of the Congo, celebrating Freedom 250, and promoting American innovation and commercial ties between the two nations.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Federally Recognized Tribes Extension Program
rows.shortSummary: The Federally Recognized Tribes Extension Program (FRTEP) provides funding to support Extension programs that deliver education and research-based knowledge to Tribal communities. The program aims to improve quality of life and economic well-being through informal education and outreach activities tailored to address unique Tribal needs.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Must serve Tribal communities and lands

rows.title: Rural Health and Safety Education Competitive Grants Program
rows.shortSummary: The Rural Health and Safety Education Competitive Grants Program provides funding to support quality of life improvements in rural communities through education and development initiatives. The program fosters coordination among federal agencies, governments, institutions, and private organizations to develop and disseminate information about rural conditions and successful rural development programs.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Tribal Colleges Research Grants Program
rows.shortSummary: The Tribal Colleges Research Grants Program supports research capacity development at tribal colleges in agriculture, natural resources, and human sciences. The program prepares students for competitive agricultural careers through leadership development and innovative technologies including AI and data science.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by posted deadline
Review full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) for detailed requirements

rows.title: Regional Food System Partnership Fiscal Year 2026
rows.shortSummary: The USDA Agricultural Marketing Service offers grants to support partnerships that connect public and private resources to plan and develop local and regional food systems. This program is part of the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP) and aims to strengthen regional food system infrastructure.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Partnership model connecting public and private resources
Focus on local and regional food system development

rows.title: Species Recovery Grants to States (Section 6 Program)
rows.shortSummary: Federal grants to eligible state agencies supporting conservation and recovery of endangered or threatened species under the Endangered Species Act, with a focus on species under NOAA's National Marine Fisheries Service jurisdiction. Funding supports cooperative efforts between federal and state governments to protect listed species in state waters and their habitats.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible state agency applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application submission
Focus on species listed as endangered or threatened under the Endangered Species Act

rows.title: Farmers Market Promotion Program Fiscal Year 2026
rows.shortSummary: The USDA Farmers Market Promotion Program provides grants to support the development and expansion of direct producer-to-consumer agricultural markets. The program aims to increase access to locally and regionally produced agricultural products through farmers market infrastructure and coordination.
keyRequirements: Must be an eligible applicant as defined in the opportunity listing
Application must be submitted through Grants.gov by the posted deadline
SAM.gov registration required prior to application
Must demonstrate focus on direct producer-to-consumer markets
